document.source_title: Pune-based chemistry lecturer involved in NEET exam is source of paper leak, CBI says

claims.claim_text: Kulkarni dictated questions during special coaching classes that matched the NEET-UG 2026 exam.
claims.evidence_text: Kulkarni dictated the questions along with options and the correct answers during these special coaching classes, and the questions were handwritten by students in their notebooks and have exactly tallied with the actual question paper of NEET-UG 2026 examination held on May 3.

entities.name: Kulkarni
entities.description: A chemistry lecturer involved in the NEET exam paper leak.
entities.evidence_text: Kulkarni was a chemistry lecturer at the Dayanand Junior College in Latur.

entities.name: Dayanand Junior College
entities.description: The college where Kulkarni taught before retirement.
entities.evidence_text: Kulkarni was a chemistry lecturer at the Dayanand Junior College in Latur.

entities.name: Manisha Waghmare
entities.description: An accomplice of Kulkarni in organizing special coaching classes.
entities.evidence_text: The CBI also arrested one Manisha Waghmare from Pune.

entities.name: Ministry of Education
entities.description: The government body involved in the investigation.
entities.evidence_text: The CBI is also learnt to have asked the Ministry of Education to provide a list of persons involved in the procedure of preparing the question papers.

events.event: CBI registered a case regarding the paper leak.
events.evidence_text: The agency had registered a case on May 12 following a written complaint from the Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education.
the actual question paper of NEET-UG 2026 examination held on May 3.

citations.title: Pune-based chemistry lecturer involved in NEET exam is source of paper leak, CBI says

source_credibility.reason: The source is a reputable news outlet providing detailed information on a significant legal case.

geolocation.name: Pune
geolocation.evidence_text: Following his arrest in Pune, Kulkarni was being taken to Delhi.
